task_completion.description: Verify the survey title, description, and end URL are configured correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the survey title 'Social Media Use and Psychological Well-Being Study 2024', a description containing 'IRB Protocol #2024-PSY-0847', and the End URL 'https://sampleuniversity.edu/research/participation-credit' in the survey overview or text elements settings.

task_completion.description: Verify the welcome message contains informed consent and the end message contains a debriefing statement.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Welcome message containing keywords like 'voluntary', 'withdraw', 'risk', 'anonymous', and the emails 'smitchell@sampleuniversity.edu' and 'irb@sampleuniversity.edu'. Look for an End message containing a hypothesis explanation and the PI's email.

task_completion.description: Verify the presentation and participant settings match the requirements.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for Presentation settings showing Format as 'Group by Group', Progress bar as 'On' (or enabled), and Backward navigation/Back button as 'Off' (or disabled). Look for Participant settings showing 'Anonymized responses' set to 'On'.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the three required question groups.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the survey structure tree or group list for exactly or at least these three groups: 'Informed Consent', 'Social Media Usage', and 'Psychological Well-Being'.

task_completion.description: Verify the correct number and distribution of questions, including the mandatory consent question.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the survey structure to count the questions: Group 1 must have at least 1 question marked as mandatory (often indicated by a red asterisk or mandatory toggle), Group 2 must have at least 2 questions, and Group 3 must have at least 2 questions (minimum 5 questions total).

task_completion.description: Verify that the survey has been activated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a status indicator showing the survey is 'Active' (e.g., a green checkmark, 'Status: Active' text, or a button that says 'Stop this survey' instead of 'Activate this survey') on the survey overview page.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the LimeSurvey web interface to create the survey.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the LimeSurvey admin dashboard in a web browser, not terminal commands, API scripts, or database interfaces.

integrity.description: Verify the questions contain actual relevant content rather than placeholder text.
integrity.visual_evidence: Question text in the 'Social Media Usage' and 'Psychological Well-Being' groups should relate to habits and the WHO-5 index respectively, rather than generic text like 'Question 1' or 'Test'.

integrity.description: Verify the UI looks genuine and not manipulated via browser developer tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The LimeSurvey interface should have consistent styling, proper alignment, and standard menus without visual artifacts suggesting HTML manipulation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Title Keywords: 'social media', 'well-being'. Required Consent Keywords in Welcome Message: 'voluntary', 'withdraw', 'risk', 'anonymous', 'smitchell@sampleuniversity.edu', 'irb@sampleuniversity.edu'. Required Debrief Keywords in End Message: 'thank', 'social media', 'smitchell@sampleuniversity.edu'. Expected End URL: 'sampleuniversity.edu/research/participation-credit'. Minimum Groups: 3 ('Informed Consent', 'Social Media Usage', 'Psychological Well-Being'). Minimum Questions: 5 (1 mandatory in G1, 2 in G2, 2 in G3). Note: All domains, emails, and IRB protocols are synthetic.
